text.title: Prompt Cache Prefix Order Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every prompt-cache-prefix-order spec MUST enforce.

rule.statement: Prompt sections MUST be ordered: system prompt → tool definitions → static context → RAG/dynamic → user input. Stable first, volatile last.
rule.rationale: Cache hits require byte-identical prefix; volatile content in the middle breaks all subsequent cached tokens.

rule.statement: cache_control breakpoint MUST be placed at the boundary between the longest stable run and the first volatile section.
rule.rationale: Breakpoint placement controls what is cacheable; placing it inside volatile content yields zero cache hits.

rule.statement: Stable prefix MUST be at least 1024 tokens (Anthropic minimum) before placing a breakpoint.
rule.rationale: Below the minimum, cache_control is ignored; no benefit.

rule.statement: No more than 4 cache_control breakpoints per request.
rule.rationale: Anthropic API caps at 4; the 5th is rejected.

rule.statement: The spec MUST emit a monitor schedule capturing cache_read_input_tokens vs cache_creation_input_tokens; alert on hit rate < target.
rule.rationale: Without monitoring, ordering drift silently kills the cache.
